en.summary: Imam al-Sadiq (AS) provides comprehensive instructions for visiting the public bathhouse (hammam), combining specific supplications with practical health advice. The teaching was given in the context of the traditional multi-chambered public baths of the early Islamic period, where each room had a different temperature. The Imam integrates spiritual purification (removing hypocrisy, seeking refuge from the self's evil) with physical hygiene advice (hot water for the head, cold water only for the feet), reflecting Islam's holistic approach to bodily and spiritual well-being.

key_terms.الحمام: Hammam - the traditional public bathhouse with multiple heated chambers
key_terms.ربقة النفاق: The yoke/bond of hypocrisy - metaphor for the constraining effect of insincerity
key_terms.الرجس النجس: The filthy impurity - both physical and spiritual uncleanliness
key_terms.الفقاع: Fuqqa' - a fermented barley drink common in medieval Islamic lands
key_terms.التقوى: Taqwa - God-consciousness, piety, awareness of the divine

en.seo_question: What are the Islamic supplications and etiquette for entering a bathhouse according to Imam al-Sadiq?

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الصُّوفِيُّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا حَمْزَةُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الْوَزَّانُ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ الْأَهْوَازِيِّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ ابْنِ حُمْرَانَ قَالَ قَالَ الصَّادِقُ جَعْفَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِمَا السَّلَامُ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: إِذَا دَخَلْتَ الْحَمَّامَ فَقُلْ فِي الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَنْزِعُ ثِيَابَكَ اللَّهُمَّ انْزِعْ عَنِّي رِبْقَةَ النِّفَاقِ وَثَبِّتْنِي عَلَى الْإِيمَانِ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتَ الْبَيْتَ الْأَوَّلَ فَقُلِ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَأَسْتَعِيذُ بِكَ مِنْ أَذَاهُ وَإِذَا دَخَلْتَ الْبَيْتَ الثَّانِيَ فَقُلِ اللَّهُمَّ أَذْهِبْ عَنِّي الرِّجْسَ النَّجِسَ وَطَهِّرْ جَسَدِي وَقَلْبِي وَخُذْ مِنَ الْمَاءِ الْحَارِّ وَضَعْهُ عَلَى هَامَتِكَ وَصُبَّ مِنْهُ عَلَى رِجْلَيْكَ وَإِنْ أَمْكَنَ أَنْ تَبْلَعَ مِنْهُ جُرْعَةً فَافْعَلْ فَإِنَّهُ يُنَقِّي الْمَثَانَةَ وَالْبَثَّ فِي الْبَيْتِ الثَّانِي سَاعَةً فَإِذَا دَخَلْتَ الْبَيْتَ الثَّالِثَ فَقُلْ نَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ النَّارِ وَنَسْأَلُهُ الْجَنَّةَ تُرَدِّدُهَا إِلَى وَقْتِ خُرُوجِكَ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ الْحَارِّ وَإِيَّاكَ وَشُرْبَ الْمَاءِ الْبَارِدِ وَالْفُقَّاعِ فِي الْحَمَّامِ فَإِنَّهُ يُفْسِدُ الْمَعِدَةَ وَلَا تَصُبَّنَّ عَلَيْكَ الْمَاءَ الْبَارِدَ فَإِنَّهُ يُضْعِفُ الْبَدَنَ وَصُبَّ الْمَاءَ الْبَارِدَ عَلَى قَدَمَيْكَ إِذَا خَرَجْتَ فَإِنَّهُ يَسُلُّ الدَّاءَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ فَإِذَا لَبِسْتَ ثِيَابَكَ فَقُلِ اللَّهُمَّ أَلْبِسْنِي التَّقْوَى وَجَنِّبْنِي الرَّدَى فَإِذَا فَعَلْتَ ذَلِكَ أَمِنْتَ مِنْ كُلِّ دَاءٍ

narrators.ambiguity_note: A narrator from whom al-Shaykh al-Saduq transmits directly.
narrators.known_identity: Al-Husayn ibn Ali al-Sufi, a direct teacher of al-Shaykh al-Saduq

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Hamza ibn al-Qasim al-Alawi al-Abbasi, though other narrators with similar names exist in Shia hadith collections.

narrators.known_identity: Ja'far ibn Muhammad ibn Malik al-Fazari al-Kufi

narrators.ambiguity_note: A less frequently cited narrator; identification is based on his position in this chain between Ja'far ibn Muhammad ibn Malik and Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Ahwazi.
Likely Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Ahwazi, a Kufi narrator; not to be confused with other narrators named Yahya ibn Sa'id.

narrators.name_ar: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ
narrators.name_en: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr
narrators.known_identity: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr al-Bazanti (d. 221 AH), a prominent companion of Imam al-Rida (AS)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Could refer to Muhammad ibn Humran ibn A'yan, a companion of Imam al-Sadiq (AS).

narrators.known_identity: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)

key_phrases.phrase_en: I seek refuge in You from the evil of my self
We seek refuge in Allah from the Fire

similar_content_hints.description: Al-Kafi contains several hadiths from Imam al-Sadiq about the etiquette of entering the bathhouse (hammam), including specific supplications and health guidance.
Multiple narrations in Shia hadith collections describe supplications for dressing and undressing, linking the physical act of clothing to spiritual garments of taqwa.
Other hadiths from the Imams provide health advice related to hot and cold water usage, bladder health, and dietary precautions in various settings.
